title: Psychotria heterochroa
intro: Psychotria heterochroa, also known as Wild Cinnamon, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria heterochroa include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of teas, tinctures, and ointments to harness its health benefits.
